title: Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Bihar

overview.short: Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Udaipur), containing Udaypur Lake (also spelled Udaipur Lake, not to be confused with Udaipur City's Five lakes) which is a Ramsar site, is
overview.description: Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Udaipur), containing Udaypur Lake (also spelled Udaipur Lake, not to be confused with Udaipur City's Five lakes) which is a Ramsar site, is a wildlife sanctuary located in West Champaran district of Bihar state, India. It was established in 1978, and covers an area of 8.74 km2. The wildlife sanctuary is predominantly 319 ha wetland, located on an oxbow lake in the floodplain of the Gandaki River.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Bettiah
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Bettiah (Latin: Bettiahen(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Bettiah in the ecclesiastical province of Patna in India. Some of the communicants within this diocese are Bettiah Christians, one of the oldest ethnoreligious Christian communities in the northern Indian subcontinent.

topPlaces.name: Bettiah
topPlaces.description: Bettiah is a city and the administrative headquarters of West Champaran district (Tirhut Division) in the Indian state of Bihar. It is near the Indo-Nepal border, 225 kilometres (140 mi) northwest of Patna.

items.note: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Bettiah (Latin: Bettiahen(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Bettiah in the ecclesiastical province of Patna in India. Some of the communicants within this diocese are Bettiah Christians, one of the oldest ethnoreligious Christian communities in the northern Indian subcontinent.

Bettiah is a city and the administrative headquarters of West Champaran district (Tirhut Division) in the Indian state of Bihar. It is near the Indo-Nepal border, 225 kilometres (140 mi) northwest of Patna.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (151 km) and the nearest railway station is Gorakhpur Junction (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Udaypur Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Diocese of Bettiah, Bettiah.
